verdict: High. Chef scores [object Object] / 100 on AI displacement exposure over a 5-year horizon.

timeline.description: O*NET task list for SOC 35-1011.00 (Chefs and Head Cooks)

key_tasks: Monitor sanitation practices to ensure that employees follow standards and regulations.
Check the quality of raw or cooked food products to ensure that standards are met.
Prepare and cook foods of all types, either on a regular basis or for special guests or functions.

evidence_ledger.description: O*NET task list for SOC 35-1011.00 (Chefs and Head Cooks)

reasoning_steps: Given the high score on ai_capability_gap, it is clear that AI can already perform many tasks currently done by chefs.
However, the regulatory_moat and physical_dexterity dimensions indicate that there are still aspects of the job that require human expertise and physical ability.

adjacent_careers.name: Food Critic
adjacent_careers.description: A food critic is a professional who evaluates and critiques restaurants, dishes, and culinary techniques.

adjacent_careers.name: Culinary Instructor
adjacent_careers.description: A culinary instructor teaches cooking classes and workshops to individuals or groups.

action_plan.description: Take a course in food safety and sanitation to improve skills in this area.
action_plan.impact: Improves employability in the culinary industry

action_plan.description: Consider pursuing a certification in culinary arts to demonstrate expertise.

follow_up_suggestions.name: Research food safety regulations and best practices
follow_up_suggestions.description: Stay up-to-date on the latest food safety guidelines and regulations.

follow_up_suggestions.name: Network with other chefs and culinary professionals to learn about job opportunities and industry trends.
follow_up_suggestions.description: Build relationships in the culinary industry to stay informed about job openings and industry developments.

recent_developments.headline: Anthropic Economic Index maps real AI usage across the workforce
recent_developments.impact: Use is concentrated in writing, coding, and analysis — physical and interpersonal work see far less AI involvement.

recent_developments.headline: OECD: about 60% of jobs in advanced economies are exposed to AI
recent_developments.impact: Exposure does not mean replacement — most exposed roles are expected to be transformed rather than eliminated.

recent_developments.headline: Labor market shifts toward AI-complementary skills
recent_developments.impact: Postings increasingly ask for judgment, verification, and direction of AI tools — not just task execution.
